# CWE-1204 negative: random IV per encryption (pure stdlib)
lang: go
file: CWE-1204-safe.pure.go
variant: pure-go
---
package sample

// CWE-1204 mitigated: crypto/rand fills a unique IV for each ciphertext.

import (
	"crypto/aes"
	"crypto/cipher"
	"crypto/rand"
	"encoding/base64"
	"io"
	"net/http"
)

func EncryptFieldSafePure(key []byte) http.HandlerFunc {
	return func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		if err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {
			http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusBadRequest)
			return
		}
		plain := r.FormValue("value")
		block, err := aes.NewCipher(key)
		if err != nil {
			http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)
			return
		}
		iv := make([]byte, block.BlockSize())
		if _, err := io.ReadFull(rand.Reader, iv); err != nil {
			http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)
			return
		}
		mode := cipher.NewCBCEncrypter(block, iv)
		padded := pkcs7PadSafePure([]byte(plain), block.BlockSize())
		out := make([]byte, len(padded))
		mode.CryptBlocks(out, padded)
		payload := append(iv, out...)
		encoded := base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(payload)
		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
		w.Write([]byte(`{"ciphertext":"` + encoded + `"}`))
	}
}

func pkcs7PadSafePure(data []byte, blockSize int) []byte {
	pad := blockSize - len(data)%blockSize
	padding := make([]byte, pad)
	for i := range padding {
		padding[i] = byte(pad)
	}
	return append(data, padding...)
}
